title: RGMCET College Overview
content: Rajeev Gandhi Memorial College of Engineering and Technology (RGMCET), Nandyal, was inaugurated on 09 March 1996 by Late Sri P. V. Narasimha Rao and Sri N. Chandrababu Naidu. The institute is one of the leading engineering colleges in Andhra Pradesh and has permanent affiliation with JNTUA Anantapuramu. RGMCET was granted Autonomous status by UGC in 2010 and continuously updates its curriculum to meet industry and societal needs.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET College Overview | Category: about | Subcategory: overview | Keywords: RGMCET, engineering college Nandyal, RGMCET overview, JNTUA affiliation, autonomous college | Rajeev Gandhi Memorial College of Engineering and Technology (RGMCET), Nandyal, was inaugurated on 09 March 1996 by Late Sri P. V. Narasimha Rao and Sri N. Chandrababu Naidu. The institute is one of the leading engineering colleges in Andhra Pradesh and has permanent affiliation with JNTUA Anantapuramu. RGMCET was granted Autonomous status by UGC in 2010 and continuously updates its curriculum to meet industry and societal needs.

title: RGMCET Accreditations and Certifications
content: All eligible undergraduate programs at RGMCET have been accredited multiple times by NBA, most recently under the Tier-I category. The institute is accredited by NAAC with an A+ grade (CGPA 3.54/4.0). RGMCET is ISO 9001:2008 certified, recognized by DSIR for research equipment benefits, and participated in the World Bank assisted TEQIP-I program for quality improvement.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Accreditations and Certifications | Category: about | Subcategory: accreditation | Keywords: RGMCET NAAC A+, NBA Tier-I, ISO 9001:2008, TEQIP, DSIR recognition | All eligible undergraduate programs at RGMCET have been accredited multiple times by NBA, most recently under the Tier-I category. The institute is accredited by NAAC with an A+ grade (CGPA 3.54/4.0). RGMCET is ISO 9001:2008 certified, recognized by DSIR for research equipment benefits, and participated in the World Bank assisted TEQIP-I program for quality improvement.

title: RGMCET Institutional Achievements
content: The Jawahar Knowledge Center at RGMCET has been awarded Best JKC Center twice by the Government of Andhra Pradesh for highest campus placements. The institute hosts the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya KAUSHAL Kendra and was recognized as a College with Potential for Excellence by UGC in 2016. RGMCET is also recognized as a SIEMENS Technical Skill Development Institute in association with APSSDC.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Institutional Achievements | Category: about | Subcategory: overview | Keywords: RGMCET achievements, Best JKC Center, UGC CPE, SIEMENS TSDI, skill development RGMCET | The Jawahar Knowledge Center at RGMCET has been awarded Best JKC Center twice by the Government of Andhra Pradesh for highest campus placements. The institute hosts the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya KAUSHAL Kendra and was recognized as a College with Potential for Excellence by UGC in 2016. RGMCET is also recognized as a SIEMENS Technical Skill Development Institute in association with APSSDC.

title: RGMCET Vision
content: The vision of RGMCET is to develop the rural-based institution into a center of technical education with global standards, to become an institute of excellence serving societal needs, and to promote value-based education with the goal of Education for Peace and Progress.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Vision | Category: about | Subcategory: vision_mission | Keywords: RGMCET vision, Education for Peace and Progress, college vision, technical education vision | The vision of RGMCET is to develop the rural-based institution into a center of technical education with global standards, to become an institute of excellence serving societal needs, and to promote value-based education with the goal of Education for Peace and Progress.

title: RGMCET Mission
content: RGMCET aims to build world-class undergraduate and postgraduate programs, create a strong research and innovation environment, transform graduates into entrepreneurs, strengthen industry–institute collaboration, and offer demand-driven courses while promoting human values and ethics.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Mission | Category: about | Subcategory: vision_mission | Keywords: RGMCET mission, engineering education mission, industry institute interaction, entrepreneurship education | RGMCET aims to build world-class undergraduate and postgraduate programs, create a strong research and innovation environment, transform graduates into entrepreneurs, strengthen industry–institute collaboration, and offer demand-driven courses while promoting human values and ethics.

title: RGMCET Quality Policy
content: RGMCET is committed to continuously improving teaching and learning processes, regularly evaluating student performance, developing centers of excellence for research and consultancy, and preparing students to compete globally while serving the nation responsibly.

title: RGMCET College Identity and Portals
content: Rajeev Gandhi Memorial College of Engineering and Technology (RGMCET) is an autonomous institution approved by AICTE, New Delhi, and affiliated to JNTUA Anantapuramu. The counselling code of the institution is RGIT. Important online portals include the Exams Portal, Faculty Login, Student Login, and Alumni Login.

title: Academic Programs at RGMCET
content: RGMCET offers undergraduate, postgraduate, doctoral, and certification programs across engineering, science, and management disciplines.

title: Undergraduate Engineering Programs at RGMCET
content: RGMCET offers B.Tech programs in Civil Engineering (intake 120), Electrical and Electronics Engineering (120), Mechanical Engineering (120), Electronics and Communication Engineering (240), Computer Science and Engineering (420), CSE Data Science (240), CSE Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (240), and CSE Cyber Security (120).

title: Postgraduate Programs at RGMCET
content: RGMCET offers postgraduate programs including Computer Aided and Structural Engineering (18), Electrical Drives and Control (18), Energy Engineering (9), VLSI Design (18), Computer Science (9), MBA (60), and MCA (60).

title: RGMCET Admissions Procedure
content: Admissions to RGMCET programs are conducted through state and national level entrance examinations in accordance with government and university regulations. B.Tech admissions are handled by the Andhra Pradesh EAMCET convener based on state-wide ranks and reservation policies. M.Tech admissions are based on GATE or PGECET ranks, while MBA and MCA admissions are based on ICET or PGECET merit. Ph.D admissions are governed by JNTUA regulations.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Admissions Procedure | Category: admissions | Subcategory: process | Keywords: RGMCET admission process, EAMCET admission RGMCET, PGECET GATE admission, MBA MCA admission RGMCET, PhD admission JNTUA | Admissions to RGMCET programs are conducted through state and national level entrance examinations in accordance with government and university regulations. B.Tech admissions are handled by the Andhra Pradesh EAMCET convener based on state-wide ranks and reservation policies. M.Tech admissions are based on GATE or PGECET ranks, while MBA and MCA admissions are based on ICET or PGECET merit. Ph.D admissions are governed by JNTUA regulations.

title: RGMCET Fee Structure
content: The tuition fee at RGMCET for the academic years 2023-24 to 2025-26 is fixed by the Andhra Pradesh Higher Education Regulatory and Monitoring Commission (APHERMC). The annual fee is ₹76,010 for B.Tech, ₹69,080 for M.Tech, ₹51,300 for MBA, and ₹60,000 for MCA programs.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Fee Structure | Category: admissions | Subcategory: fees_scholarships | Keywords: RGMCET fees, BTech fee RGMCET, MBA MCA fee, APHERMC fee structure, engineering college fees Nandyal | The tuition fee at RGMCET for the academic years 2023-24 to 2025-26 is fixed by the Andhra Pradesh Higher Education Regulatory and Monitoring Commission (APHERMC). The annual fee is ₹76,010 for B.Tech, ₹69,080 for M.Tech, ₹51,300 for MBA, and ₹60,000 for MCA programs.

title: RGMCET Scholarships and Fee Reimbursement
content: RGMCET provides multiple scholarship and fee reimbursement opportunities. Students admitted with EAMCET rank below 10,000 receive full tuition fee waiver for four years. The Government of Andhra Pradesh offers tuition fee reimbursement to eligible students. GATE-qualified M.Tech students are eligible for AICTE stipend, and PGECET or ICET admitted students may receive government fee reimbursement subject to eligibility.

Students admitted with EAMCET rank below 10,000 receive full tuition fee waiver for four years. The Government of Andhra Pradesh offers tuition fee reimbursement to eligible students. GATE-qualified M.Tech students are eligible for AICTE stipend, and PGECET or ICET admitted students may receive government fee reimbursement subject to eligibility.

title: RGMCET Central Library Overview
content: The RGMCET Central Library is housed in a fully air-conditioned three-storied building with a large collection of books, journals, digital resources, and internet facilities for students and faculty.

floors.ground_floor: Book issue section including SC/ST book bank collections.
floors.first_floor: Reference section with encyclopedias, dictionaries, and prescribed textbooks for in-library use.
floors.second_floor: Journals section, digital library, discussion rooms, staff reading room, and video rooms.

title: RGMCET Library Digital Resources
content: RGMCET Central Library provides access to major digital resources including IEEE Xplore, Taylor and Francis, NLIST, DELNET, J-Gate Plus, National Digital Library of India, and NPTEL video lectures.

title: Library Working Hours and Borrowing Rules
content: The Central Library operates on all working days with defined borrowing and circulation policies.
text_for_embedding: Library Working Hours and Borrowing Rules | Category: campus_life | Subcategory: library | Keywords: RGMCET library timings, library working hours, library borrowing policy, library fine rules, central library schedule | The Central Library operates on all working days with defined borrowing and circulation policies.

title: Library General Rules and Regulations
content: Library users must follow strict discipline and borrowing rules while using the RGMCET Central Library.
rules: Wearing ID card is compulsory inside the library.
Entry in visitors register is mandatory.
No eatables or drinks allowed inside.
Books will be issued subject to availability only.
No sub-lending of books is permitted.
Personal books or printed material are not allowed inside.
Reference books, newspapers, magazines, and journals cannot be taken outside.
loss_policy: Loss of books must be reported immediately to the librarian.
Borrower must replace the lost book with a similar copy.
If not replaced in time, twice the cost plus fine will be charged.
text_for_embedding: Library General Rules and Regulations | Category: campus_life | Subcategory: library | Keywords: RGMCET library rules, library discipline rules, library ID card required, library silence policy, library book loss policy | Library users must follow strict discipline and borrowing rules while using the RGMCET Central Library.

title: RGMCET College Transportation Services
content: RGMCET operates college bus services from major nearby towns to the campus on all working days. Students opting for transportation must pay the prescribed bus fee at the beginning of each academic year.
service_notes: Bus services operate on all working days.
Students must pay transportation fees as per college norms.
Routes cover major towns around Nandyal.

title: Civil Engineering Department Overview
content: The Civil Engineering department at RGMCET was established in 2008. It offers B.Tech in Civil Engineering with an intake of 120 and M.Tech in Computer Aided Structural Engineering with an intake of 18. The department provides quality education supported by experienced faculty, modern laboratories, and strong infrastructure.

It offers B.Tech in Civil Engineering with an intake of 120 and M.Tech in Computer Aided Structural Engineering with an intake of 18. The department provides quality education supported by experienced faculty, modern laboratories, and strong infrastructure.

title: Civil Engineering Vision and Mission
content: The department aims to provide value-based civil engineering education that produces socially responsible professionals. It focuses on innovation, research orientation, leadership, teamwork, and strong industry collaboration.

title: Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) Department Overview
content: The Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) program in Computer Science and Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) was introduced in the academic year 2022–2023 with an intake of 60 students. Artificial Intelligence enables machines to simulate human intelligence and solve complex problems using algorithms and data. Machine Learning focuses on building systems that can learn patterns from data and make predictions automatically. The program prepares students with strong foundations in artificial intelligence, machine learning, data analytics, and intelligent system development for modern industry applications.

The program is designed to combine core computer science knowledge with business and management skills. It focuses on the practical implementation of computer science concepts in real-world business applications. Students gain strong foundations in computing while also developing knowledge in humanities, economics, management sciences, and ethical values.

title: Computer Science and Engineering (Cyber Security) Department Overview
content: The Bachelor of Technology program in Computer Science and Engineering with specialization in Cyber Security was introduced in the academic year 2022–2023. The program focuses on developing expertise in secure systems, cyber defense mechanisms, and information protection technologies. Students gain hands-on practical experience in secure system design, network security, cryptography, web security, and cyber risk management. The curriculum also emphasizes legal, behavioral, and ethical aspects of cybersecurity to prepare graduates capable of protecting digital infrastructure and developing secure computing solutions.

title: RGMCET Examination Malpractice Rules
content: RGMCET enforces strict disciplinary action for examination malpractice. Students found with unauthorized materials, copying, impersonation, misbehavior, or disrupting examinations may face penalties such as expulsion from the exam hall, cancellation of results, or debarment from future examinations depending on the severity of the offence.
key_offences: Possession of unauthorized materials such as notes, mobile phones, or programmable devices in the exam hall.
Copying or receiving or giving assistance during examinations.
Smuggling answer scripts or question papers.
Using abusive or offensive language in answer scripts.
Misbehavior or disturbance in the examination hall.
Taking away or tearing answer scripts.
Possessing lethal weapons in the exam hall.
Appearing for exams in drunken condition.
major_penalties: Cancellation of performance in one or more subjects.
Debarment for two consecutive semesters in serious cases.
Forfeiture of seat in severe violations.

Students found with unauthorized materials, copying, impersonation, misbehavior, or disrupting examinations may face penalties such as expulsion from the exam hall, cancellation of results, or debarment from future examinations depending on the severity of the offence.

title: RGMCET Training and Placement Cell Overview
content: The Training and Placement (T&P) Cell at RGMCET operates through a structured committee including the Placement Officer, faculty coordinators, and student representatives from each department. The cell prepares annual training plans to improve student employability and bridge the gap between academic learning and industry requirements through training programs, alumni seminars, campus recruitment drives, internships, and soft-skill development sessions.
objectives: Provide awareness to students about industry-required skills.
Reduce the gap between academics and industry needs.
Conduct alumni seminars from reputed MNC professionals.
Organize technical and employability skill sessions.

title: RGMCET Internship and Industrial Training Guidelines
content: RGMCET provides structured guidelines for students pursuing internships during summer vacation. Students may apply to industries or reputed institutes such as IITs, IISc, IIITs, NITs, IIMs, and ISB. Internships must be relevant to the student's engineering branch and typically carry academic credit.
key_rules: Minimum internship duration is three weeks and carries 2 credits (not counted for CGPA).
Students may apply individually or in teams of up to four members.
Students must obtain a recommendation letter from the Head of Department.
Consent letter from the host organization must be submitted at least one month before the next semester begins.
Students must follow the rules and code of conduct of the host organization.
Students bear expenses for travel, food, and accommodation unless stipend is provided.
Students must submit an internship report after completion.
text_for_embedding: RGMCET Internship and Industrial Training Guidelines | Category: placements | Subcategory: internships | Keywords: RGMCET internship rules, industrial training RGMCET, summer internship guidelines, internship credits RGMCET, engineering internship policy | RGMCET provides structured guidelines for students pursuing internships during summer vacation. Students may apply to industries or reputed institutes such as IITs, IISc, IIITs, NITs, IIMs, and ISB. Internships must be relevant to the student's engineering branch and typically carry academic credit. | key rules: Minimum internship duration is three weeks and carries 2 credits (not counted for CGPA)., Students may apply individually or in teams of up to four members., Students must obtain a recommendation letter from the Head of Department., Consent letter from the host organization must be submitted at least one month before the next semester begins., Students must follow the rules and code of conduct of the host organization., Students bear expenses for travel, food, and accommodation unless stipend is provided., Students must submit an internship report after completion.

title: Internship Project Work and Evaluation
content: Internship project work at RGMCET is jointly supervised by an internal faculty member and an external industry supervisor. Student performance is evaluated based on quality of work, learning ability, attendance, and professional behavior.
evaluation_rules: Students must work under both internal and external supervisors.
Students must stay in contact with the internal supervisor during the internship.
External supervisor or HR evaluates performance and sends report to the college.
Students who fail to secure minimum required marks must repeat the final semester.
Project report signed by both supervisors must be submitted to the department.
A completion certificate from the host organization must be included.
Students must also submit a brief internship experience report.

title: RGMCET Internship Eligibility Criteria
content: To be eligible for internships at RGMCET, students must maintain a minimum CGPA of 6.5 without active backlogs and secure at least 75% attendance in each semester.
criteria: Minimum CGPA of 6.5 without active backlogs at the end of III Year II Semester.
Minimum 75% attendance in every semester up to IV Year I Semester.
